Working Capital Facility Agreement
The Facility is for £1,000,000 in total, is unsecured, interest free and the Company was able to be drawn down in four loan tranches of £250,000 each and the Company has made two Loan Tranche drawdowns of £250,000 each under the Facility and is not permitted to make any additional drawdowns. To date £50,000 has been paid by the Lender which is due to be repaid to the Lender. The Facility was created as a standby facility and the Company is re-negotiating the terms of the Facility with the Lender who is a long term shareholder in the Company.
Repayment and Conversion
Repayment
Unless otherwise converted, the Company must repay each Loan Tranche on the first anniversary of the advance by the Lender of the applicable Loan Tranche ("
Maturity Date
"). The Company may prepay the whole or part of the Facility on any day prior to the Maturity Date for a Loan Tranche upon giving not less than 14 days' prior written notice to the Lender and paying in cash a prepayment fee of 5% of the amount which the Company prepays in cash before the Maturity Date. The Lender can during the 14 days' notice period make an election for all or part of the Loan subject to a prepayment notice to be repaid in Shares in which case the 5% fee shall not apply to that proportion of the Loan repaid in Shares.
Conversion of Loan Tranche by Lender
The Lender may at any time during the Facility Period elect to convert all or part of any drawn down amount into such number of new Shares equal to the amount of the Loan Tranche that is to be repaid at the date of the election divided by the conversion price. The original conversion price was 2.8 pence ("
Original Conversion Price
") which under the conversion adjustment mechanism described below has been reduced to 1.2727 due to the fundraising at 1 pence per share announced by the Company on 10 February 2025 ("
February 25 Fundraising
") ("
New Conversion Price
").
Conversion of Loan by the Company
The Company may at any time during the Loan Period elect to convert all or part of a Loan if the Share price exceeds a target conversion price for a period of five or more business days. The original target conversion price was 3.6 pence per share (
"Original Target Conversion Price
") which under the conversion adjustment mechanism described below has been reduced to 1.6362 pence following the February 2025 Fundraising ("
New Target Conversion Price
").
Conversion Adjustment Mechanism
If the Company before i) the Maturity Date for a Loan Tranche and before ii) the Loan Tranche has been repaid issues Shares for cash consideration ("
Issue Price
") at a discount to 2.2 pence per Share (the "
Base Issue Price
") then the Conversion Price and the Target Conversion Price in respect of that Loan Tranche shall be multiplied
by a fraction, the numerator of which will be the Issue Price and the denominator of which will be 2.2 pence.
Interest and Fees
The Loan is interest free. The Lender shall be paid an arrangement fee of 10% of the amount of the Facility to be settled by the issue of 5,089,177 new Shares ("

Drawdown Fee Shares
") with the Drawdown Fee Shares to be issued on or before 31 December 2024 or such other date agreed by the parties. The Drawdown Fee Shares have not yet been issued.
Option to Extend Facility
If the Company had drawn down in full or in part against all four loan tranches then it had the option to elect to be able to drawdown up to an additional GBP500,000 ("

No Draw Down Warrants

company_30s.what_they_do: African Pioneer is a mining exploration and development company focused on copper and gold projects in Africa, mainly the Ongombo project in the Democratic Republic of Congo.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Copper is a cornerstone of the global transition to electric vehicles and renewable energy, while gold remains a key safe‑haven asset. African Pioneer’s project sits in a region with rich mineral deposits and growing infrastructure investment, positioning it to tap a high‑growth resource market.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the exploration and licensing phase, having just secured a mining licence and raised funds through a recent equity offering. It has yet to produce any revenue or ore, and its cash burn is high. The stock is trading very cheaply, reflecting the high risk and early‑stage nature of the business.

rns_narrative: The company has been actively raising capital and issuing shares to fund its exploration program. In the last few months it secured a mining licence for the Ongombo copper‑gold project, which is the first regulatory step toward potential production.

Financially, the firm posted a modest loss in its latest interim results and disclosed a £1.8m fundraising round that diluted existing shareholders. The most recent RNS also announced a new holding by a director, indicating ongoing internal interest.
These events suggest the company is still building its operational foundation rather than generating cash flow, and it remains heavily dependent on future financing and commodity price movements.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the recent mining licence and capital raise as signs that the company is moving toward a production timeline, and they hope a rise in copper prices will unlock value.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears argue that the firm has no revenue, a thin operating history, and a high dilution risk. They worry that without a clear path to profitability the stock could continue to slide.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community has stayed largely silent; when chatter does appear it is neutral or slightly bullish, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, indicating no public interest—perfect for a contrarian play.

near_term_catalysts: A formal production start announcement from the Ongombo project would signal that the company is moving beyond exploration.
An increase in global copper prices above $10,000 per tonne could make the project economically attractive and boost investor confidence.
A subsequent equity offering or a strategic partnership that brings in experienced mining operators could improve the company’s execution credibility.
A positive regulatory update, such as a full mining licence approval or a tax incentive, would reduce operating uncertainty.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden jump in trading volume that accompanies a positive RNS, a price break above the recent 20‑day moving average, or a copper price spike that lifts the broader mining sector.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early phase of a deep crash, with the APEX score suggesting a tightening of price action. Confirmation of mean reversion will likely come after a clear catalyst—such as a production announcement—has been priced in.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is trading at a fraction of its historical peak, and the company has secured the first regulatory step toward production. If copper prices rise and the project becomes viable, a bounce could lift the share price several times its current level, delivering a 3–5× return before the next cycle of volatility.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without revenue, high dilution, and a long path to production, the stock could remain illiquid or fail to recover. A continued decline could wipe out the investment if the price falls to zero.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: You could lose the entire investment if the share price collapses to zero, but a recovery to 20–30p would represent a 3–5× gain. The asymmetry favors the upside if the catalyst materialises.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a dip near the 10p support level with a position size that limits exposure to a single trade. Set a stop just below the 10p support (e.g., 8p). Scale into the position if volume spikes and the price holds above 12p.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until you see a clear volume surge or a copper price push above $10,000/mt. If either occurs, consider a partial entry at 12–14p with a tight stop at 10p.
playbook.if_passing: If the company fails to secure a full mining licence, misses production milestones, or is delisted, exit immediately to avoid further loss.

ai_insights.bottom_line: The deep crash, combined with a licensing milestone and potential copper price rally, creates a high‑reward, high‑risk contrarian play that could deliver a 3–5× return if the project moves to production.
